content:
# Session 87 — iter-087 review

## Metadata

- **Archon iteration**: 087
- **Stage**: prover (single substantive lane — `Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean`).
- **Sorry count before iter-087** (BasicOpenCech only): 6 syntactic sorries — L502, L614 (substep a infrastructure), L854 (extra-degeneracy on s₀), L1478 (`h_diff_pi_smul_f`), L1523 (`g_R.map_smul'`), L1552 (`h_loc_exact`). Pre-iter-087 absolute lines per session 86 PROJECT_STATUS.
- **Sorry count after iter-087** (BasicOpenCech only): **6** syntactic sorries — L522, L614, L938, L966, L1156, L1185 (lines re-shifted by the iter-087 refactors). Net **0** change.
- **Active sorry count in project (all files, excl. references/)**: 47 string occurrences across .lean files (some are inside doc-comments / commentary). Active syntactic sorry sites unchanged from iter-086: BasicOpenCech 6, Differentials 5, Modules/Monoidal 1, Jacobian 1, Picard/Functor 1 → 14 total.
- **Compilation status iter-087**: **NOT VERIFIED IN SANDBOX**. The prover and refactor subagents all reported `lake build` / `lean_diagnostic_messages` returning `permission denied (error code: 13)` because `.lake/packages/*` is owned by `root`, not `archon`. Hence every `lean_diagnostic_messages` call in iter-087 returned `success: false` with empty items. **Compilation must be re-verified in the dispatcher environment before iter-088 plan accepts the iter-087 work as landed.**
- **Env state** (from `attempts_raw.jsonl` summary line):
  49 total events; **2 source edits** (both to BasicOpenCech.lean, replacing one inline `sorry` block with the S1–S5 prefix); **6** `lean_goal` (4 returned empty goals — the file was sorry-only at those positions); **3** `lean_diagnostic_messages` (all clean — but `success: false` per the sandbox issue; clean = empty items, not validated); **1** `lean_build` (failed `permission denied`); **2** lemma searches (`piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype` confirmed at `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Products.lean:63`).
- **`lean_verify`**: not run this iteration.

---

## Lane summary

| Lane | File | Status | Sorry Δ | Compile |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | **STRUCTURE TRANSCRIBED (compilation unverified in sandbox)** — the iter-086 verified S1–S5 prefix (12-arg `intro` + `funext j` + `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` + `hRel` + `dsimp only [...]` + `simp [...]` + `rw [show ... = piIsoPi Z₂/Z₁]` + `show (πZ₂).hom _ j = r • (πZ₂).hom _ j` + double `piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply`) was transcribed into the new top-level theorem `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` body at L495–L522. The trailing `sorry` at L522 is the S6 distribution chain (a–h) per the iter-086 recipe. No S6 attempt was made (LSP non-functional in sandbox; user policy forbids long-form in-code scaffolding). | 0 (6 → 6) | UNVERIFIED |
| — | refactor subagent `basicopencech-extract-pi-smul` (plan-agent-driven, ran before prover) | COMPLETE | 0 | unverified |
| — | refactor subagent `basicopencech-specialize-pi-smul` (plan-agent-driven, ran after extract) | COMPLETE — soundness fix on extract's universal signature | 0 | unverified |

---

## Lane 1 — `BasicOpenCech.lean`: structure transcribed; closure not attempted

**Status**: STRUCTURE TRANSCRIBED. The prover landed the S1–S5 prefix in the body of the new top-level theorem `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` (created by the iter-087 plan-agent refactor subagent). The trailing `sorry` is the S6 distribution chain awaiting the inline `hom_sum_dist` (per the iter-086 recipe).

### Concrete delivery — S1–S5 prefix transcription (events 34, 35 in JSONL)

The body of `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` at L495–L522 was replaced from `sorry` (single line) to the following 27-line tactic block followed by a single `sorry`:

```lean
intro R K₀ scK₀ Z₁ Z₂ e₁ e₂ perI₁ h_mod_pi₁ perI₂ h_mod_pi₂ r y
funext j
simp only [Pi.smul_apply]
have hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1 = n := by
  rcases n with _ | k
  · simp at hn
  · simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]
dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor,
  toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]
simp [FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor,
  FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor,
  AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex,
  AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj,
  AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD,
  AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.map,
  CochainComplex.of, FormalCoproduct.evalOp,
  CosimplicialObject.δ, e₁, e₂, ModuleCat.piIsoPi, dif_pos hRel]
rw [show (limit.isoLimitCone
      ⟨ModuleCat.productCone Z₂, ModuleCat.productConeIsLimit Z₂⟩)
      = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂ from rfl,
    show (limit.isoLimitCone
      ⟨ModuleCat.productCone Z₁, ModuleCat.productConeIsLimit Z₁⟩)
      = ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₁ from rfl]
show (ConcreteCategory.hom (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂).hom) _ j =
    r • (ConcreteCategory.hom (ModuleCat.piIsoPi Z₂).hom) _ j
rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply,
    ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply]
sorry
```

The two edits were sequential: edit 1 (event 34) attempted a more elaborate version with comment lines and a (truncated) larger tactic block; edit 2 (event 35) replaced edit 1 with the cleaner transcription above. Both edits succeeded at the file-write level; `lean_diagnostic_messages` event 36 reported `clean: true` (BUT `success: false` per the sandbox — the diagnostics endpoint cannot validate in this environment, see the env state note in Metadata).

### Attack record (iter-087) — Lane 1

| Event | Tactic / Action | Result | Insight |
|---|---|---|---|
| 14 | `lean_goal` at `BasicOpenCech.lean:495` baseline (pre-edit) | `goals: null, goals_before: [], goals_after: []` | confirms the position is at a `sorry` line where the proof has been closed — but the empty `goals` reflects the sandbox LSP failure mode, not actual completion |
| 15 | `lean_goal` at L494 | `line_context: r • e₂ (⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom scK₀.f) (e₁.symm y)) := by` | confirms the signature ends at L494 |
| 16, 17 | `lean_goal` at L495 col 2/3 (post-edit attempt) | empty goals | sandbox LSP cannot return real goals |
| 18, 19 | `lean_diagnostic_messages` x2 | clean (0 errors / 0 warnings, but `success: false` per sandbox) | sandbox returns "clean" trivially when LSP cannot load |
| 21 | `lean_build` | `success: false, permission denied (error code: 13)` x N | mathlib oleans owned by `root`; sandbox cannot build |
| 28 | `grep` for `h_diff_pi_smul_f`/`R_restrict_R_linear` in iter-086 step-009 snapshot | confirmed iter-086 inline `have` form starts at L1013 of step-009 | grounds the S1–S5 transcription in the verified-iter-086 source |
| 34 | `Edit` — first transcription attempt (~30 LOC) | wrote OK | iter-086 body adapted for new top-level signature; includes a leading comment line and adapts the per-bind setup |
| 35 | `Edit` — second transcription (clean 27 LOC, no comment scaffolding) | wrote OK | adheres to the user no-scaffolding policy: replaces the original `sorry` with S1–S5 + trailing single `sorry`, no inline iter-NNN comments |
| 36 | `lean_diagnostic_messages` | `errors: [], clean: true` (BUT `success: false`) | unverifiable in sandbox |
| 39, 40 | `lean_local_search` `piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply` / `piIsoPi_hom_ker` | found `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype` at `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Products.lean:63` | confirms the `_apply` form (used in the edit) is auto-generated via `@[elementwise]` on the underlying lemma |
| 41 | `grep` in Mathlib `Products.lean` | `theorem piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype (i : ι) :` at L63 | exact location verified |
| 43, 44 | `grep` sorries in edited file | L522, L614, L938, L966, L1156, L1185 (6 sites) | confirms hard cap 6 respected |
| 48 | `Write` task result | written ✓ | task result delivered |

### Adaptation notes (vs. iter-086 inline-have form)

- The iter-086 inline body (in `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`'s proof) had additional `letI := h_mod_X₁`, `letI := h_mod_X₂`, an inline `R_restrict_R_linear` `have`, and an `hsmul_eq` rewrite. In the new top-level theorem context (post-refactor), `R_restrict_R_linear` has been extracted to top-level `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` (iter-087 refactor), and the conclusion now uses `h_mod_pi₁`/`h_mod_pi₂` directly — so `hsmul_eq` is **not needed** because the smul on both sides is already in the `Pi.module` form (no AddEquiv.module transport in the way).
- The 12-arg `intro` peels the 4 `let` bindings (`R`, `K₀`, `scK₀`, `Z₁`, `Z₂`), the 2 `let` equivalences (`e₁`, `e₂`), the 4 `letI` Module instances (`perI₁`, `h_mod_pi₁`, `perI₂`, `h_mod_pi₂`), and the 2 ∀-binders (`r`, `y`).

### Iter-088 Lane 1 path forward (concrete, from prover task result)

Goal after the S1–S5 prefix:
```
(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv (r • y))) =
  r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom ((eqToHom ∘ₗ Σ.hom) ((piIsoPi Z₁).inv y))
```

S6 distribution chain (a–h):
- (a) inline `hom_sum_dist : ∀ s f, ModuleCat.Hom.hom (∑ i ∈ s, f i) = ∑ i ∈ s, ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f i)` via `Finset.cons_induction` + `ModuleCat.hom_add` + base case (`ModuleCat.hom_sum` does NOT exist by direct name in Mathlib — confirmed iter-086).
- (b) `rw [hom_sum_dist]` on both sides.
- (c) distribute `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom` over the outer sum via `LinearMap.map_sum` / `Finset.sum_apply`.
- (d) per-`(i,j)`: `Pi.lift_π_apply` to peel `Pi.lift`.
- (e) `Pi.smul_apply` on `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_i.toOrderHom)` to surface inner R-action.
- (f) `RingHom.map_mul` to split product `restrict_{i→j}(r-restricted * y(j∘δ_i))`.
- (g) `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` (the iter-087 extracted top-level lemma at L425) to collapse the algebra-map chain `R = Γ(U) → Γ(V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(V_j)`.
- (h) reassemble via `Finset.smul_sum` to match outer `r •` on RHS.

Estimated ~40–60 LOC of carefully-sequenced tactics, the bulk being the `hom_sum_dist` inline have (~6 LOC).

### Risks documented by the iter-087 prover

1. **`intro` on `let`/`letI` conclusion.** If Lean elaborator beta-reduces the lets before `intro` fires, the 12-arg `intro` line will fail. Recovery: drop the leading `intro` and use `simp only` to unfold lets, or call `intros r y` after lets reduce. Status: not validated in sandbox.
2. **`Pi.smul_apply` firing.** Depends on `[∀ i, SMul R (Z₂ i)]` synthesizable from `perI₂`. `perI₂` is in scope post-`intro`. Recovery: `letI := perI₂` before the `simp only`.

---

## Refactor subagent activity (plan-agent dispatched, pre-prover)

The iter-087 plan agent invoked two refactor subagents on the BasicOpenCech.lean structure before the prover ran. Both completed; both share the same compilation-not-verified caveat (sandbox issue).

### Refactor 1 — `basicopencech-extract-pi-smul`

**Action**: extracted the inline `have h_diff_pi_smul_f` block (~466 lines including iter-073→iter-086 dead-end scaffolding) inside `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` into a new top-level theorem `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`. Also extracted the inline `have R_restrict_R_linear` (iter-086, fully proved) into a new top-level lemma `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`.

**Result**: top-level lemma `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` landed (L412–L434, fully proved, 3-tactic body — byte-for-byte the iter-086 body). Top-level theorem `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` initial version landed with a `sorry` body. **First-pass signature was universally-false** (over arbitrary `scK₀_f` / `Z₁` / `Z₂` / `e₁` / `e₂` / `h_mod_pi_i`).

### Refactor 2 — `basicopencech-specialize-pi-smul`

**Action**: replaced the universally-false abstract `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` (former L466–L481) with a **concrete signature** specialized to the project's Čech-cochain context (explicit args `hU : IsAffineOpen U`, `s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)`, `hn : 0 < n`; let-bound `R`/`K₀`/`scK₀`/`Z₁`/`Z₂`/`e₁`/`e₂` reproduced inside the theorem).

**Result**: concrete `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` at L455–L495 with body `sorry`. Call site at L1084 updated from `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul (R := R) e₁ e₂ h_mod_pi₁ h_mod_pi₂ scK₀.f` to `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul hU s₀ hn`. The downstream `h_diff_pi_smul_f r (e₁ x)` rewrite in `f_R.map_smul'` continues to type-check by definitional equality of the let-bound quantities (refactor subagent verified-by-inspection; sandbox-validated nope).

This refactor sequence is an **iter-087 honesty fix**: refactor 1's abstract signature would have been a false axiom (claims `e₂ (scK₀_f.hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) = r • e₂ (scK₀_f.hom (e₁.symm y))` for any morphism `scK₀_f`, which is false). The mathematical-honesty audit protocol (added iter-086 after the `_root_.SheafOfModules.exact_iff_stalkwise` debacle) caught it before iter-088, and refactor 2 specialized the statement to the provable concrete case.

---

## Aggregate findings & iter-088 outlook

- **Net sorry count unchanged**. The single `sorry` at the body of `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` was replaced with the S1–S5 prefix + a trailing `sorry`, so the BasicOpenCech.lean count holds at 6 syntactic sorries.
- **Iter-087 structural delivery (compilation unverified)**: the inline `have h_diff_pi_smul_f` block (a ~466 LOC nest of iter-073→iter-086 commentary) has been replaced with a clean two-line application of a new top-level theorem `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`. The genuine iter-086 inline `have R_restrict_R_linear` is hoisted to top-level `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`. The hot proof obligation is now a focused top-level goal — the same goal that previously sat ~1000 lines into `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`.
- **Iter-087 mathematical-honesty save**: the first-pass extract refactor produced a universally-false signature. The specialize refactor caught it. No false axiom landed in the source. **The iter-086 audit protocol functioned as designed.**
- **Lane 1 path forward unchanged from iter-086**: the S6 distribution chain (a–h) with inline `hom_sum_dist`. iter-087 produced no new dead-ends because no S6 attempt was made (LSP non-functional in sandbox — the prover correctly declined to write a long-form scaffolding comment per user policy).
- **Realistic iter-088 target**: net **−1** sorry (5 active in BasicOpenCech, 13 total) — best case if the S6 chain lands cleanly in the now-focused top-level theorem context. The path forward is concrete, the supporting infrastructure (`presheafMap_restrict_collapse`) is in scope at top-level, and the S1–S5 prefix has been transcribed. The only remaining risks are (1) the sandbox LSP issue (must be resolved or the iter-088 prover must run in a different environment), and (2) the two prover-flagged transcription risks (`intro` on lets, `Pi.smul_apply` synth).

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)

No manual marker changes this iter.

- The relevant chapter is `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § Čech acyclicity (sec L751). `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` is a project-local helper extracted by the iter-087 refactor; it does not have its own `\lean{...}` macro entry (it is supporting infrastructure for the main `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` theorem, which itself still has its existing `\lean{...}` macro untouched).
- No `\mathlibok` adds/removes: nothing landed on a Mathlib-direct alias this iter.
- No `\lean{...}` macro corrections: no prover-side declaration rename this iter (the new top-level `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` and `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` are project-local helpers, not blueprint-declared statements).
- No `% NOTE:` additions: the iter-087 sandbox-LSP issue is documented in the prover task result and in this summary; it is not a translation gap in a blueprint statement.
- No `\notready` strips: nothing landed.
- The deterministic `sync_leanok` phase ran prior to this review — its `\leanok` placements/removals stand untouched.

## TO_USER status

The iter-087 prover and both refactor subagents flagged the same sandbox blocker: `.lake/packages/*` directories are owned by `root` rather than `archon`, causing `lake build` and `lean_diagnostic_messages` to fail with `permission denied (error code: 13)`. This is a pre-existing environmental condition (documented in iter-086 and the iter-087 plan + refactor reports). It is surfaced via `.archon/TO_USER.md` this iteration so the user can address it before iter-088 — otherwise iter-088's S6 closure attempt will be similarly unverifiable. See `.archon/TO_USER.md`.
